data.title: U.S. Natural Gas Futures Appear to Stabilize — Market Talk
data.description: U.S. natural gas futures show signs of stabilizing after suffering losses on a cooler temperature outlook and lower LNG feedgas flows. The bounce off mid-week lows "and the ability to maintain this bottom into early next week could signal that additional gains lie ahead," Ritterbusch says in a note. The weather factor continues to rule, however, and cooler-than-normal temperatures in the first week of August and possibly the following week should limit upside. "Buying interest will likely be quelled by the late stage of the cooling cycle," the firm adds. Nymex natural gas settles up 0.5% at $3.110/mmBtu for a 13% weekly loss. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

data.title: Wheat Futures Fall on Global Supply Pressure
data.description:
Wheat futures dipped below $5.40 a bushel on Friday, ending the week lower as ample global supply outweighed support from strong US export demand.

Despite the price decline, US wheat exports remained solid, with net sales for the week ended July 17 totaling 712,000 metric tons—at the high end of trade expectations.

Ongoing Northern Hemisphere harvests continued to add pressure, boosting global supply and keeping prices in check.

In the US, scouts on a North Dakota crop tour projected hard red spring wheat yields to average 49.0 bushels per acre, down from 54.5 bpa last year.

While North Dakota is a key producer, nearby states are seeing mixed crop conditions.

Minnesota’s crop is in near-record condition, offering some optimism, while Montana’s crop—once among the worst—has shown signs of improvement.

data.title: ICE Review: Canola Goes Its Own Way
data.description:
WINNIPEG, Manitoba--Canola futures on the Intercontinental Exchange were higher on Friday despite weakness in most comparable oils.

Chicago soyoil and Malaysian palm oil were down, while European rapeseed was mostly up. Crude oil declined due to the Trump administration allowing Chevron to resume operations in Venezuela.

Scattered showers and thunderstorms are expected on the Canadian Prairies today and this weekend. Central and southern Alberta should see the heaviest precipitation.

At mid-afternoon, the Canadian dollar was down more than four-tenths of a United States cent compared to Thursday's close.

There were 32,543 canola contracts traded on Friday, which compares with Thursday when 33,337 contracts changed hands. Spreading accounted for 17,126 of the contracts traded.

Settlement prices are in Canadian dollars per metric tonne.

data.title: GrafTech Gains on Cost-Cutting Initiatives, 2Q Sales Growth
data.description:
By Nicholas G. Miller

Shares of GrafTech climbed Friday after the company posted higher second-quarter sales volume and said its cost-cutting initiatives would lead to future profitability.

The stock was up 14%, to $1.54, midday Friday. It is still down 12% so far this year.

The graphite electrode manufacturer reported sales-volume growth of 12% for the second quarter, fueled by a 38% increase in U.S. sales volume. The company also said it reduced cash costs per metric ton of production by 13% and increased its full-year cost-cutting guidance.

Chief Executive Tim Flanagan said the company "is executing cost savings initiatives that will accelerate our return to normalized profitability as the market recovers."

On the company's earnings call, Flanagan said the company had significantly improved its "cost structure from fixed and variable operating expenses to corporate overhead costs."

The Brooklyn Heights, Ohio, company reported positive EBITDA for the first time since the second quarter a year ago.

Write to Nicholas G. Miller at nicholas.miller@wsj.com

data.title: MINERALS TECHNOLOGIES INC SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Minerals Technologies Inc., a global leader in minerals-based technology solutions, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of 2025. The report highlights the company's financial performance and operational activities, providing insights into its strategic initiatives and market dynamics.

Financial Highlights

  • Net Sales: $528.9 million, a decrease of 2% compared to $541.2 million in the prior year.
  • Production Margin: $136.9 million, representing 25.9% of sales, down from 26.6% in the prior year.
  • Income from Operations: $74.6 million, an increase of 47% compared to $50.6 million in the prior year.
  • Net Income: $46.3 million, an increase of 122% compared to $20.9 million in the prior year.
  • Net Income Attributable to Minerals Technologies Inc.: $45.4 million, an increase of 130% compared to $19.7 million in the prior year.
  • Diluted EPS: $1.44, compared to $0.61 in the prior year, reflecting a significant improvement in earnings per share.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: The Consumer & Specialties segment reported net sales of $277.7 million for the three-month period ended June 29, 2025, with Household & Personal Care sales slightly increasing to $127.4 million, while Specialty Additives sales decreased by 5% to $150.3 million. The Engineered Solutions segment saw a 2% decrease in net sales to $251.2 million, with High-Temperature Technologies sales down by 3% and Environmental & Infrastructure sales up by 1%.
  • Geographical Performance: Net sales in the United States slightly increased to $281.9 million, while international sales decreased to $247.0 million, reflecting a 5% decline from the previous year. This indicates a stronger performance in the domestic market compared to international regions.
  • Operational Challenges: The company faced higher operating costs in the Consumer & Specialties segment, primarily due to unfavorable volume leverage and product mix, impacting income from operations, which decreased by 23% compared to the prior year.
  • Restructuring Activities: The company recorded a $5.8 million charge for restructuring and other items, primarily related to the consolidation of two facilities, which impacted operational efficiency and cost structure.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to focus on innovation and new product development, aiming to increase market share in global pet litter products, deploy new products in pet care, and expand its presence in the calcium carbonate market. Additionally, the company is looking to enhance its operational excellence and explore selective acquisitions to strengthen its core competencies.
  • Environmental Initiatives: The company continues to develop products and processes for waste management and recycling opportunities, aiming to reduce the environmental impact for its customers by improving the sustainability of their products.
  • Market Expansion: The Engineered Solutions segment is focusing on increasing its presence in emerging markets with bentonite-based foundry solutions and expanding its refractory maintenance model to other steel makers globally.
  • Product Development: The company is actively developing new mineral-based solutions for personal care applications and expanding its bentonite product solutions for animal health applications, indicating a strategic focus on diversifying its product offerings.

SEC Filing: MINERALS TECHNOLOGIES INC [ MTX ] - 10-Q - Jul. 25, 2025

data.title: Oil Futures Post Back-to-Back Weekly Losses — Market Talk
data.description: Oil futures end the session and the week lower in rangebound trade, with tariff concerns tempered by the U.S. reaching several trade deals and moving toward an agreement with the EU. "We're in a peak summer lull, I think there's just not much of an appetite for putting on significant directional risk," says Ben Hoff of Societe Generale. "The crude market has a fair bit of human psychology baked into it, and we've just come off a period of massively elevated geopolitical risk with pretty significant price swings," he says, referring to the Israel-Iran conflict. The next catalyst will likely be more anchored in fundamentals. "We're quite bearish from a build perspective for 4Q25 and into 2026, but the upside risks have also increased somewhat." WTI settles down 1.3% at $65.16 a barrel and Brent falls 1.1% to $68.44, for weekly losses of 1.3% and 1.2%, respectively. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

data.title: Law firm Milbank cuts Katyal’s $3,250 rate in defending NJ cities against Trump
data.description:
Prominent lawyer Neal Katyal of law firm Milbank will charge a sharply reduced hourly rate and not his standard $3,250 as he helps to defend two New Jersey cities sued by President Donald Trump’s administration over their immigration policies, according to a contract obtained by Reuters.

Milbank's lawyers, including Katyal, will each charge $300 an hour — a fee that will be split evenly by the cities of Newark and Hoboken for the litigation in federal court in New Jersey, the contract showed.

The Trump administration in May sued Newark, Hoboken and two other New Jersey cities, accusing them of being so-called sanctuary jurisdictions and obstructing federal immigration agents.

Katyal in the July 11 contract said he normally charges $3,250 an hour, and that some other partners at his 1,000-lawyer firm bill hourly between $1,865 and $2,475.

New York-headquartered Milbank is one of nine firms that reached deals with Trump in March and April, after he began issuing executive orders against law firms that restricted their access to government officials and federal contracting work.

Milbank, which had not been hit with an order, in its deal said it earmarked $100 million in free legal services for mutually agreed-upon initiatives with the White House.

Four other law firms successfully sued to block Trump's orders against them.

Here, Milbank in defending the two cities said it agreed to reduce its standard rates because a third-party — the nonprofit Goodnation Foundation — is picking up part of the legal tab. Hoboken said it will not pay more than $53,000 in any calendar year to Milbank.

In a statement on Friday, the Goodnation Foundation said it may help underwrite legal defense "in select cases where the public interest is served and community needs are paramount."

The foundation said it does not typically disclose details of its funding, which it said comes from "donors committed to advancing access to justice."

Milbank and Katyal did not immediately respond to requests for comment. A Newark official had no immediate comment, and Hoboken did not immediately respond to a request for one.

Large law firms commonly reduce their hourly rates, or sometimes bill at no cost, for legal work for state and local government clients.

As hourly rates have steadily increased across the profession, at least two other law firms, Quinn Emanuel Urquhart & Sullivan and Susman Godfrey, also have hit the $3,000 an hour milestone.

A January report by the Thomson Reuters Institute and the Georgetown Law Center on Ethics and the Legal Profession said clients have been willing to accept “dramatic increases” in rates. The institute and Reuters share the same parent company.

Katyal, a former acting U.S. solicitor general during President Barack Obama's administration, in 2022 was billing at $2,465 an hour when he was at law firm Hogan Lovells. He joined Milbank in February, and now leads the firm’s appellate team. He has argued more than 50 cases before the U.S. Supreme Court.

Milbank's team defending Newark and Hoboken also includes Gurbir Grewal, who is a former New Jersey attorney general and enforcement head at the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission, and appellate specialist Colleen Roh Sinzdak.

Read more:

US judge slashes fees for IBM lawyers in billion-dollar software contract fight

Lawyers in banking cases take a loss despite $35 million fee award

US judge rejects lawyers' $94 million fee bid in auto parts pricing case

Lawyers defend $205 million legal fee in US auto class action settlement

More lawyers join the $3,000-an-hour club, as other firms close in

data.title: Dow Jones Top Markets Headlines at 3 PM ET: U.S., EU Trade Deal Nearing As Trump Visits Scotland  | S&P ...
data.description:
U.S., EU Trade Deal Nearing As Trump Visits Scotland

Trump and Ursula von der Leyen, a top EU top official, are set to meet Sunday during president's golf trip.

----

S&P 500 on Pace for Fifth Straight Record Close

The S&P 500 and Nasdaq composite reached fresh highs, following a busy week of tariff updates and earnings.

----

U.S. Durable Goods Orders Sink on Lower Aircraft Orders

Total orders for goods made to last at least three years, such as automobiles, planes and electronic equipment, were 9.3% lower in June than in May, Commerce Department data said Friday. Economists polled by The Wall Street Journal expected an 11.1% slump.

----

Trump's Tariffs Are Being Picked Up by Corporate America

Neither consumers nor foreign countries are assuming much of the tariff burden. At least not yet.

----

Tokyo Inflation Signals Easing Cost Pressure Ahead of BOJ Meeting

Consumer inflation in the Tokyo metropolitan area eased slightly in July, but stubbornly high food prices continue to strain household budgets.

----

German Business Sentiment Edges Higher Despite Tariff Uncertainty

The increase was a sign of resilience against U.S. tariffs, though uncertainty kept any upswing in check.

----

U.K. Retailers Get Summer Boost as Drinks Sales Soar

Retail sales were 0.9% higher on month in June, with alcoholic and nonalcoholic drinks among the products which saw higher sales.

----

Russia's Central Bank Cuts Key Rate Again as Economy Slows

Friday marked the second-straight time the Bank of Russia cut its interest rate after it lowered borrowing costs in early June.

----

ECB Must Remain Completely Open in Future Rate Decisions, Says Villeroy

Policymakers must remain agile, noting that the stronger euro was having a significant disinflationary effect, Bank of France Governor Francois Villeroy de Galhau said.

----

Week Ahead for FX, Bonds: Fed Decision, U.S. Jobs Data, Tariff Deadline in Focus

A U.S. Federal Reserve interest-rate decision; President Trump's August 1 deadline for reciprocal tariffs; and key data on U.S. jobs, economic growth and inflation will take center stage in a busy week for markets.

----

Trump Is Shifting to Dealmaking Mode on China

The White House puts a priority on doing business with Beijing as it prepares for bilateral talks next week.

----

U.K. Consumers Are Feeling Gloomier Amid Foundering Economy, Rising Prices

U.K. consumer sentiment worsened as the country faces an unhappy combination of weak growth and still-high inflation.

----

The More Trump Pressures the Fed, the Less Likely He Gets Lower Rates

If the president thinks the Fed is hard to deal with, wait until he tries to negotiate with the bond market.

----

Resilient Trump Lifted by Improved View of the Economy, WSJ Poll Finds

The president's job rating is holding steady despite dim views of his tax-and-spending law, tariff policies and the government's handling of the Jeffrey Epstein investigation.

data.title: Dow Jones Top Company Headlines at 3 PM ET: LVMH in Talks to Sell Marc Jacobs  | Volkswagen ...
data.description:
LVMH in Talks to Sell Marc Jacobs

The luxury giant has been holding talks with the owner of Reebok, among others.

----

Volkswagen Teases Made-in-America Audis After $1.5 Billion Tariff Hit

The German automaker said it expects challenges due to political uncertainty, expanding trade restrictions and geopolitical tensions.

----

Centene Reports Loss, Citing Rising Medical Costs

The managed-care company pointed to increased medical spending in Medicaid, due to behavioral health, home health and high-cost drugs.

----

Charter Communications' Modest Growth, Broadband Loss Triggers Steep Sell-Off

Charter logged lower-than-expected earnings and tepid revenue growth in its latest quarter, sparking a sell-off among investors.

----

Meta to Halt Political Advertising in EU, Citing Transparency Rules

Meta said its decision won't affect political advertising outside of the EU and that EU users will still be able to debate politics.

----

Booz Allen Posts Mixed Results as Fed Crackdown Weighs

Booz Allen posted mixed results in its fiscal first quarter, a period of time in which the company previously said it expected some disruption as the Trump administration cracked down on federal contracting.

----

LG Electronics Profit Slips as Tariffs Bite

The South Korean consumer-electronics giant said its television segment struggled with sluggish demand and higher marketing expenses.

----

AutoNation Stock Dips Even After the Car Seller Grows Every Sales Segment

The car retailer's same-store revenue in the second quarter increases 8% from the prior year.

----

HCA Lifts Outlook After Quarterly Jumps in Revenue, Profit

HCA Healthcare raised its full-year guidance for earnings and sales as both figures exceeded analyst targets for the second quarter.

----

Puma CEO Plans Brand Reset After Profit Warning Sends Shares Tumbling

The sporting-goods company is working to soften the blow from tariffs, refresh its product lineup, reduce excess inventory and rein in costs.

----

Airlines Are Having a Bruising Year. Delta and United Are Doing Better.

American and Southwest have been more reliant on shakier domestic travel.

----

Remy Cointreau Softens Tariff Hit Estimate Despite Widespread Trade Tensions

The company said customs duties will be less than anticipated, at a time when the industry is embroiled in trade disputes in key markets.

----

FCC Approves Paramount's $8 Billion Merger With Skydance

The signoff on the deal, which could close next month, follows Paramount's settlement of a Trump lawsuit.

----

Intel to Lay Off 15% of Workers, Cancel Billions in Projects in Bid for Rebound

The American chip-making giant is facing a strategic crossroads as it chases rivals like Nvidia and TSMC in the global silicon boom.

data.title: Tea App, Meant to Protect Women, Faces Security Breach — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Angela Palumbo

Tea — a viral new app that's meant to make women feel safe while using online dating services — has been subject to a security breach that exposed users' IDs.

Tea confirmed the breach to Barron's. In a statement, the company said that it identified unauthorized access to one of its systems and immediately launched an investigation to assess the impact. Preliminary findings indicate that the incident involved a legacy data storage system containing information from over two years ago, Tea said.

Tea added that approximately 72,000 images — including about 13,000 images of selfies and photo identification submitted during account verification and 59,000 images publicly viewable in the app from posts, comments and direct messages — were accessed without authorization.

"Tea has engaged third-party cybersecurity experts and are working around the clock to secure our systems," the company said. "At this time, there is no evidence to suggest that current or additional user data was affected."

According to a 2023 study by Pew Research, 56% of women under 50 who have used dating sites or apps say they've been sent a sexually explicit message or image they didn't ask for, and about four-in-ten have had someone continue to contact them after they said they weren't interested.

Tea was launched in 2023, and has recently become the most downloaded free app in Apple's U.S. App Store, after gaining recent popularity on social media. According to researcher Sensor Tower, downloads of the Tea app through the first 23 days of July were up 320% compared to the same period in June.

The app is meant to provide women an anonymous space to find information about the men they are interested in speaking with through dating apps. The app uses reverse image searches to catch fake profiles, phone number lookups to check for secret relationships, and background checks to look for possible criminal records.

The Tea app uses a strict onboarding procedure that asks women to register by creating a username, while providing location, birth date, a selfie, and ID photo to verify the user is a woman. After uploading that information, users have to wait for access to get into the app. Tea had received over two million requests to join the service over the past week, the app maker said on its Instagram account on Thursday.

While the promise of anonymity may have enticed users, Tea says on its Privacy Policy page that "despite our efforts, no security measures are impenetrable."

The data breach comes during a difficult time for the online dating industry. Bumble announced in June that it was laying off about 30% of its workforce after total paying users declined. Match Group — the owner of Tinder and Hinge — also announced a 13% workforce reduction in May.

Raymond James analyst Andrew Marok recently wrote that "burnout from dating apps is real."

"Whether the fatigue has been caused by the nature of dating apps themselves or external factors, it is clear that churn/retention are under threat for online dating apps as taking a break from dating serves as an emotional upside for users," Marok wrote.

Bumble and Match Group didn't immediately respond to requests for comment.

Write to Angela Palumbo at angela.palumbo@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Front Month Nymex Crude Fell 1.35% This Week to Settle at $65.16 — Data Talk
data.description:
Front Month Nymex Crude for Sept. delivery lost 89 cents per barrel, or 1.35% to $65.16 this week

  • Down for two consecutive weeks
  • Down $3.29 or 4.81% over the last two weeks
  • Largest two week net and percentage decline since the week ending July 3, 2025
  • Today it is down 87.00 cents or 1.32%
  • Down eight of the past 10 sessions
  • Lowest settlement value since Monday, June 30, 2025
  • Off 18.61% from its 52-week high of $80.06 hit Monday, Aug. 12, 2024
  • Up 14.06% from its 52-week low of $57.13 hit Monday, May 5, 2025
  • Down 15.55% from 52 weeks ago
  • Off 18.59% from its 2025 settlement high of $80.04 hit Wednesday, Jan. 15, 2025
  • Up 14.06% from its 2025 settlement low of $57.13 hit Monday, May 5, 2025
  • Off 55.15% from its record high of $145.29 hit Thursday, July 3, 2008
  • Month-to-date it is up 0.08%
  • Year-to-date it is down $6.56 or 9.15%

All prices are calculated based on the settlement price of the current front month contract.

Source: Dow Jones Market Data

data.title: The Week That Meme Stocks Returned — WSJ
data.description:
By Jack Pitcher

Social media buzzed with excitement on Monday as some beaten-down stocks that retail traders have been buying began to soar.

Kohl's, Opendoor Technologies, Krispy Kreme and Rocket Cos. were among the names that posted outsized swings throughout the week. All four companies have been the subject of attention on investing forums and the social-media platform X.

The big moves, untethered from any clear changes in the companies' outlooks, were reminiscent of the pandemic-era meme stock craze, when shares of companies like GameStop and AMC Entertainment went parabolic.

Like the first time around, individuals have been targeting businesses viewed as past-their-prime that have a high percentage of their shares sold short, an indication that Wall Street is betting they will fall.

So-called "short-squeezes," where investing firms buy back shares of companies they've bet against to get out of the losing trade, likely accelerated the gains, analysts said.

By the end of the week, some of the excitement had fizzled, and investors who bought at the peak were sitting on big losses. Opendoor shares were up more than 115% on Monday, but weekly gains had collapsed to about 10% by Friday afternoon.

Read more about the meme stock resurgence here:

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).
